title: Subjunctive with emotional verbs
objectives: Use the subjunctive after expressions of emotion
Build sentences with 'me alegro de que', 'siento que', etc.

vocab.en: I'm sorry that ... / I feel that ...

grammar_notes: Any emotional reaction + 'que' triggers the subjunctive in the next clause.
'Ojalá' (from Arabic 'in sha'allah') always takes the subjunctive: 'Ojalá venga.' (Hopefully he comes.)
Note: same subject → use infinitive with 'de'. 'Me alegro de venir' (I'm glad TO come) vs 'Me alegro de que vengas' (I'm glad YOU come).
example_phrases: Me alegro de que estés aquí.
